root_text.an10.28:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kajaṅgalāyaṁ viharati veḷuvane.
root_text.an10.28:1.2: Atha kho sambahulā kajaṅgalakā upāsakā yena kajaṅgalikā bhikkhunī tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā kajaṅgalikaṁ bhikkhuniṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho kajaṅgalakā upāsakā kajaṅgalikaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an10.28:2.2: ‘eko pañho eko uddeso ekaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ, dve pañhā dve uddesā dve veyyākaraṇāni, tayo pañhā tayo uddesā tīṇi veyyākaraṇāni, cattāro pañhā cattāro uddesā cattāri veyyākaraṇāni, pañca pañhā pañcuddesā pañca veyyākaraṇāni, cha pañhā cha uddesā cha veyyākaraṇāni, satta pañhā sattuddesā satta veyyākaraṇāni, aṭṭha pañhā aṭṭhuddesā aṭṭha veyyākaraṇāni, nava pañhā navuddesā nava veyyākaraṇāni, dasa pañhā dasuddesā dasa veyyākaraṇānī’ti.
root_text.an10.28:2.3: Imassa nu kho, ayye, bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa kathaṁ vitthārena attho daṭṭhabbo”ti?
root_text.an10.28:3.1: “Na kho panetaṁ, āvuso, bhagavato sammukhā sutaṁ sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ, napi manobhāvanīyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ sammukhā sutaṁ sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ;
root_text.an10.28:3.3: taṁ suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha, bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an10.28:3.4: “Evaṁ, ayye”ti, kho kajaṅgalakā upāsakā kajaṅgalikāya bhikkhuniyā paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an10.28:4.1: “‘Eko pañho eko uddeso ekaṁ veyyākaraṇan’ti, iti kho panetaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā.
root_text.an10.28:4.3: Ekadhamme, āvuso, bhikkhu sammā nibbindamāno sammā virajjamāno sammā vimuccamāno sammā pariyantadassāvī sammadatthaṁ abhisamecca diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhassantakaro hoti.
root_text.an10.28:4.6: imasmiṁ kho, āvuso, ekadhamme bhikkhu sammā nibbindamāno sammā virajjamāno sammā vimuccamāno sammā pariyantadassāvī sammadatthaṁ abhisamecca diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhassantakaro hoti.
root_text.an10.28:4.7: ‘Eko pañho eko uddeso ekaṁ veyyākaraṇan’ti, iti yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā idametaṁ paṭicca vuttaṁ.
root_text.an10.28:5.1: ‘Dve pañhā dve uddesā dve veyyākaraṇānī’ti iti, kho panetaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā.
root_text.an10.28:5.3: Dvīsu, āvuso, dhammesu bhikkhu sammā nibbindamāno sammā virajjamāno sammā vimuccamāno sammā pariyantadassāvī sammadatthaṁ abhisamecca diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhassantakaro hoti.
root_text.an10.28:5.8: imesu kho, āvuso, tīsu dhammesu bhikkhu sammā nibbindamāno sammā virajjamāno sammā vimuccamāno sammā pariyantadassāvī sammadatthaṁ abhisamecca diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhassantakaro hoti.
root_text.an10.28:5.9: ‘Tayo pañhā tayo uddesā tīṇi veyyākaraṇānī’ti, iti yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā idametaṁ paṭicca vuttaṁ.
root_text.an10.28:6.1: ‘Cattāro pañhā cattāro uddesā cattāri veyyākaraṇānī’ti, iti kho panetaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā.
root_text.an10.28:6.3: Catūsu, āvuso, dhammesu bhikkhu sammā subhāvitacitto sammā pariyantadassāvī sammadatthaṁ abhisamecca diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhassantakaro hoti.
root_text.an10.28:6.6: imesu kho, āvuso, catūsu dhammesu bhikkhu sammā subhāvitacitto sammā pariyantadassāvī sammadatthaṁ abhisamecca diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhassantakaro hoti.
root_text.an10.28:6.7: ‘Cattāro pañhā cattāro uddesā cattāri veyyākaraṇānī’ti, iti yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā idametaṁ paṭicca vuttaṁ.
root_text.an10.28:7.1: ‘Pañca pañhā pañcuddesā pañca veyyākaraṇānī’ti, iti kho panetaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā.
root_text.an10.28:7.3: Pañcasu, āvuso, dhammesu bhikkhu sammā subhāvitacitto sammā pariyantadassāvī sammadatthaṁ abhisamecca diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhassantakaro hoti.
root_text.an10.28:7.12: imesu kho, āvuso, aṭṭhasu dhammesu bhikkhu sammā subhāvitacitto sammā pariyantadassāvī sammadatthaṁ abhisamecca diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhassantakaro hoti.
root_text.an10.28:7.13: ‘Aṭṭha pañhā aṭṭhuddesā aṭṭha veyyākaraṇānī’ti, iti yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā idametaṁ paṭicca vuttaṁ.
root_text.an10.28:8.1: ‘Nava pañhā navuddesā nava veyyākaraṇānī’ti, iti kho panetaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā.
root_text.an10.28:8.3: Navasu, āvuso, dhammesu bhikkhu sammā nibbindamāno sammā virajjamāno sammā vimuccamāno sammā pariyantadassāvī sammadatthaṁ abhisamecca diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhassantakaro hoti.
root_text.an10.28:8.6: imesu kho, āvuso, navasu dhammesu bhikkhu sammā nibbindamāno sammā virajjamāno sammā vimuccamāno sammā pariyantadassāvī sammadatthaṁ abhisamecca diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhassantakaro hoti.
root_text.an10.28:8.7: ‘Nava pañhā navuddesā nava veyyākaraṇānī’ti, iti yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā idametaṁ paṭicca vuttaṁ.
root_text.an10.28:9.1: ‘Dasa pañhā dasuddesā dasa veyyākaraṇānī’ti, iti kho panetaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā.
root_text.an10.28:9.3: Dasasu, āvuso, dhammesu bhikkhu sammā subhāvitacitto sammā pariyantadassāvī sammadatthaṁ abhisamecca diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhassantakaro hoti.
root_text.an10.28:9.6: imesu kho, āvuso, dasasu dhammesu bhikkhu sammā subhāvitacitto sammā pariyantadassāvī sammadatthaṁ abhisamecca diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhassantakaro hoti.
root_text.an10.28:9.7: ‘Dasa pañhā dasuddesā dasa veyyākaraṇānī’ti, iti yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā idametaṁ paṭicca vuttaṁ.
root_text.an10.28:10.1: Iti kho, āvuso, yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitāsu mahāpañhāsu:
root_text.an10.28:10.3: dasa pañhā dasuddesā dasa veyyākaraṇānī’ti, imassa kho ahaṁ, āvuso, bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa evaṁ vitthārena atthaṁ ājānāmi.
root_text.an10.28:10.4: Ākaṅkhamānā ca pana tumhe, āvuso, bhagavantaññeva upasaṅkamitvā etamatthaṁ paṭipuccheyyātha.
root_text.an10.28:10.5: Yathā vo bhagavā byākaroti tathā naṁ dhāreyyāthā”ti.
root_text.an10.28:10.6: “Evaṁ, ayye”ti kho kajaṅgalakā upāsakā kajaṅgalikāya kho bhikkhuniyā bhāsitaṁ abhinanditvā anumoditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā kajaṅgalikaṁ bhikkhuniṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
root_text.an10.28:10.7: Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho kajaṅgalakā upāsakā yāvatako ahosi kajaṅgalikāya bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ kathāsallāpo, taṁ sabbaṁ bhagavato ārocesuṁ.
root_text.an10.28:11.2: Paṇḍitā, gahapatayo, kajaṅgalikā bhikkhunī. Mahāpaññā, gahapatayo, kajaṅgalikā bhikkhunī.
root_text.an10.28:11.3: Mañcepi tumhe, gahapatayo, upasaṅkamitvā etamatthaṁ paṭipuccheyyātha, ahampi cetaṁ evamevaṁ byākareyyaṁ yathā taṁ kajaṅgalikāya bhikkhuniyā byākataṁ.
root_text.an10.28:11.4: Eso ceva tassa attho. Evañca naṁ dhāreyyāthā”ti.

translation_text.an10.28:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kajaṅgalā in a bamboo grove.
translation_text.an10.28:1.2: Then several lay followers of Kajaṅgalā went to the nun Kajaṅgalikā, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to her:
translation_text.an10.28:2.1: “Ma’am, this was said by the Buddha in ‘The Great Questions’:
translation_text.an10.28:2.2: ‘One thing: question, summary recital, and answer. Two … Three … Four … Five … Six … Seven … Eight … Nine … Ten things: question, summary recital, and answer.’
translation_text.an10.28:2.3: How should we see the detailed meaning of the Buddha’s brief statement?”
translation_text.an10.28:3.1: “Good people, I haven’t heard and learned this in the presence of the Buddha or from esteemed mendicants.
translation_text.an10.28:3.2: But as to how it seems to me,
translation_text.an10.28:3.3: listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an10.28:4.1: “‘One thing: question, summary recital, and answer.’ That’s what the Buddha said,
translation_text.an10.28:4.2: but why did he say it?
translation_text.an10.28:4.3: Becoming completely disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding one thing, seeing its limits and fully comprehending its meaning, a mendicant makes an end of suffering in this very life.
translation_text.an10.28:4.5: ‘All sentient beings are sustained by food.’
translation_text.an10.28:4.6: Becoming completely disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding this one thing, seeing its limits and fully comprehending its meaning, a mendicant makes an end of suffering in this very life.
translation_text.an10.28:4.7: ‘One thing: question, summary recital, and answer.’ That’s what the Buddha said, and this is why he said it.
translation_text.an10.28:6.3: With a mind well developed in four things—seeing their limits and fully comprehending their meaning—a mendicant makes an end of suffering in this very life.
translation_text.an10.28:6.5: The four kinds of mindfulness meditation. …
translation_text.an10.28:6.6: With a mind well developed in these four things—seeing their limits and fully fathoming their meaning—a mendicant makes an end of suffering in this very life. …
translation_text.an10.28:8.3: Becoming completely disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding nine things, seeing their limits and fully comprehending their meaning, a mendicant makes an end of suffering in this very life.
translation_text.an10.28:8.5: The nine abodes of sentient beings.
translation_text.an10.28:8.6: Becoming completely disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding these nine things, seeing their limits and fully comprehending their meaning, a mendicant makes an end of suffering in this very life.
translation_text.an10.28:9.1: ‘Ten things: question, summary recital, and answer.’ That’s what the Buddha said,
translation_text.an10.28:9.2: but why did he say it?
translation_text.an10.28:9.3: Becoming well developed in ten things—seeing their limits and fully fathoming their meaning—a mendicant makes an end of suffering in this very life.
translation_text.an10.28:9.5: The ten ways of performing skillful deeds.
translation_text.an10.28:9.6: With a mind well developed in these ten things—seeing their limits and fully fathoming their meaning—a mendicant makes an end of suffering in this very life.
translation_text.an10.28:9.7: ‘Ten things: question, summary recital, and answer.’ That’s what the Buddha said, and this is why he said it.
translation_text.an10.28:10.1: That’s how I understand the detailed meaning of what the Buddha said in brief in ‘The Great Questions’.
translation_text.an10.28:10.4: If you wish, you may go to the Buddha and ask him about this.
translation_text.an10.28:10.5: You should remember it in line with the Buddha’s answer.”
translation_text.an10.28:10.6: “Yes, ma’am,” replied those lay followers, approving and agreeing with what the nun Kajaṅgalikā said. Then they got up from their seat, bowed, and respectfully circled her, keeping her on their right. Then they went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side,
translation_text.an10.28:10.7: and informed the Buddha of all they had discussed.
translation_text.an10.28:11.2: The nun Kajaṅgalikā is astute, she has great wisdom.
translation_text.an10.28:11.3: If you came to me and asked this question, I would answer it in exactly the same way as the nun Kajaṅgalikā.
translation_text.an10.28:11.4: That is what it means, and that’s how you should remember it.”

variant_text.an10.28:10.5: Yathā vo → yathā kho (mr); yathā no (bahūsu)
